title: Senior Personnel
content:
Senior personnel on the project comprise Dr. Terra López (Principal Investigator) and Dr. Sam Ng (Co-Principal Investigator). Effort and salary reflect one summer month per year for each senior person, escalated at 3% per year per institutional assumptions.

**Dr. Terra López, Principal Investigator.** Dr. López requests one summer month of effort in each of the three project years. Year 1 base salary is $175,000, escalated at 3% annually. Dr. López leads the overall scientific direction of the project, oversees the field campaigns at the California study site, directs the linking of leaf-scale optical measurements to canopy-scale remote-sensing observations, and supervises the graduate research assistants and postdoctoral researcher.

**Dr. Sam Ng, Co-Principal Investigator.** Dr. Ng requests one summer month of effort in each of the three project years. Year 1 base salary is $140,000, escalated at 3% annually. Dr. Ng co-leads the field-spectroscopy campaigns, leads the canopy-scale remote-sensing analyses, and coordinates the integration of results from the subrecipient soil-biogeochemistry laboratory with the optical measurements.

title: Other Personnel
content:
Other personnel requested on the project are one postdoctoral researcher, two graduate research assistants, and one undergraduate research assistant.

**Postdoctoral Researcher (to be named).** A full-time (100% calendar-year) postdoctoral researcher is requested for each of the three project years at a Year 1 salary of $65,000, escalated at 3% annually. The postdoc will lead day-to-day execution of the field campaigns, process field-spectroradiometer data, and contribute to integration with remote-sensing imagery.

**Graduate Research Assistants (two; to be named).** Two graduate research assistants are requested for each project year at 50% academic-year and 100% summer effort. Year 1 stipend is $32,000 per GRA, escalated at 3% annually and paid per the institution's GRA stipend schedule. Associated tuition remission is budgeted under Section G (Other Direct Costs — Other) per institutional budgeting policy. The two GRAs will conduct field measurements, contribute to data analysis, and lead first-author components of the project's publications.

**Undergraduate Research Assistant (to be named).** Funding is requested for one undergraduate research assistant at approximately 400 summer hours per year at the institution's undergraduate research rate, totaling $6,000 in Year 1 and escalated at 3% annually. The undergraduate will support field-campaign logistics and sample processing.

title: Fringe Benefits
content: Fringe benefits are charged at institutional rates applicable to each personnel category (faculty, postdoctoral researcher, graduate student, and undergraduate) in effect at the time each charge is incurred. Specific rates are those established by the institution's current federally negotiated rate agreement and are reflected in the budget form as Section C per year. Rates applied in this budget follow the institution's standard practice of applying higher fringe rates to faculty and postdoctoral personnel than to graduate students and undergraduate hourly appointments.

title: Equipment
content:
One item of equipment is requested, in Year 1 only.

**Field spectroradiometer (visible to shortwave-infrared, continuous), Year 1, $28,500.** A continuous visible-to-shortwave-infrared field spectroradiometer is required for in-situ leaf- and canopy-scale reflectance measurements at the California study site. No suitable instrument is available through the institution's shared spectroscopy facility, and loaner windows from nearby field stations do not align with the project's planned field-campaign schedule. The acquisition cost exceeds the NSF equipment threshold of $5,000 and the instrument's useful life exceeds one year, so the item is correctly classified as equipment under Section D rather than as materials and supplies under Section G.

title: Travel
content:
All project travel is domestic; no international travel is requested.

**Annual AGU meeting attendance.** In each of the three project years, funds support the PI and one graduate research assistant to attend the annual American Geophysical Union meeting to present project results. Typical per-person costs include airfare, lodging, registration, and per diem at institutional rates.

**Field campaigns at the California study site.** In each project year, funds support the PI, co-PI, postdoctoral researcher, and the two graduate research assistants to travel to the California study site for two ten-day field campaigns. Costs include airfare, ground transportation to the field site, lodging, and per diem at institutional rates.

title: Participant Support Costs
content:
Participant support costs fund a two-week summer research experience at the prime institution for six undergraduate students in each of Years 2 and 3. No participant support is requested in Year 1. Participants are undergraduate students (not project employees) and the experience is mentored by the PI, co-PI, and postdoctoral researcher.

Per-participant costs in each of Years 2 and 3 are:

- **Stipend** — 6 participants × $3,000 = $18,000
- **Travel** — 6 participants × $500 = $3,000
- **Subsistence** — 6 participants × $3,000 = $18,000 (on-campus housing and meals during the two-week program)
- **Program fee** — 6 participants × $1,000 = $6,000 (registration and materials)

Total per year: $45,000.

Consistent with NSF policy, indirect costs are not charged on participant support costs, and rebudgeting funds out of participant support into other budget categories requires prior NSF approval.

title: Other Direct Costs
content:
### Materials and Supplies

Expendable field supplies (sample bags, vials, tubing, reagents), calibration targets for the field spectroradiometer, and consumables for GPS and data-logging equipment are budgeted at approximately $6,000 per year across the three project years.

### Publication Costs

Page charges and open-access fees are budgeted in Year 3 for two peer-reviewed manuscripts projected for submission from the project's results.

### Subawards

**State University of X (Dr. Jamie Park).** A subaward is requested to State University of X in each of the three project years (Year 1: $95,000; Year 2: $98,000; Year 3: $101,000) for soil-biogeochemistry analyses — trace-gas flux measurements and isotope-ratio mass spectrometry on soil cores collected at the California study site. The work is performed at the subrecipient's established biogeochemistry laboratory, which operates instrumentation not available at the prime institution. Consistent with the prime institution's indirect-cost base description, only the first $25,000 of each subaward year is included in the MTDC base for F&A at the prime.

### Other — Tuition Remission

Tuition remission for the two graduate research assistants is budgeted per institutional rates and is accounted under NSF Other Direct Costs per institutional budgeting policy.

title: Indirect Costs
content:
Indirect costs (Facilities and Administrative costs) are charged on Modified Total Direct Costs (MTDC), which excludes equipment, participant support costs, and the portion of each subaward in excess of $25,000. Rates are drawn from the institution's current DHHS-negotiated F&A rate agreement, current through 2028.

The applicable on-campus rate steps up during the project period per the current rate agreement: 52% applies in Years 1 and 2, and 54.5% applies in Year 3. The off-campus rate, where applicable, is 26%.
